seoTitle: Integrate HubSpot With Microsoft Outlook: Setup
metaDescription: Connect Microsoft Outlook to HubSpot for bi-directional email syncing, with OAuth authorization, logging rules, and blocklist configuration.
intro: Bringing Outlook into HubSpot means your email conversations log automatically to the right contact, without manually copying anything over. This guide covers finding the email integration settings, connecting through Microsoft's OAuth flow, and setting up logging rules to keep sensitive threads private. It ends with confirming the sync is live.

steps.heading: Open Email Integrations
steps.body: Go to your HubSpot account Settings, then General Preferences, and open the Email Integrations tab.

steps.heading: Select Microsoft 365 or Exchange
steps.body: Click Connect Personal Email and choose the Microsoft 365 or Exchange integration bridge.

steps.heading: Accept ingestion permissions
steps.body: Toggle the automated inbox ingestion agreement and click Connect to proceed.

steps.heading: Complete Microsoft OAuth
steps.body: Pass through the Microsoft OAuth handshake to grant HubSpot bi-directional sync permissions.

steps.heading: Set blocklists and logging rules
steps.body: Adjust custom blocklist domains and outgoing log rules to control what gets recorded.

steps.heading: Verify the sync
steps.body: Check your connected Outlook status row and run a live synchronization check.

faq.question: Does this work with both Outlook desktop and web?
faq.answer: The integration syncs through Microsoft 365 or Exchange, so it covers accounts regardless of whether you use desktop or web Outlook.

faq.question: Can I exclude certain email addresses from logging?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set up custom blocklist domains to keep specific senders or recipients out of the sync.

faq.question: Is the sync one-way or two-way?
faq.answer: It's bi-directional, meaning emails can log into HubSpot from Outlook and vice versa depending on your settings.

faq.question: How do I confirm the Outlook connection is active?
faq.answer: Check the connected status row in your Email Integrations settings and run a live synchronization test.
